1.0 Overview
1.1 Brand Positioning

The foundation of everything we communicate. These statements define who Incisiv is, why we exist, and where we are headed — they should inform every piece of content and every brand decision.

Purpose
To empower retail and consumer industry leaders with the intelligence, insights, and connections needed to accelerate digital transformation and drive measurable business outcomes.
Vision
To be the world's most trusted intelligence platform for the retail and consumer industry — enabling leaders to make faster, smarter decisions with confidence.
Mission
To provide industry leaders with actionable research, peer connections, and strategic advisory that accelerates the adoption of transformative technologies.
1.2 Core Values

Five values that define how Incisiv operates — shaping our research, our events, and every relationship we build in the industry.

Creative
Embrace fresh ideas and innovative thinking to inspire growth and transformation in the retail and consumer industry.
Accurate
Focus on precision, clarity, and attention to detail to ensure high standards across all research, data, and communications.
Inclusive
Promote collaboration, openness, and respect to foster meaningful connections across the industry ecosystem.
Bold
Challenge the status quo and pursue ambitious goals with confidence and determination.
Reliable
Maintain consistency, accountability, and trustworthiness — the bedrock of every engagement and deliverable.
1.3 Tone of Voice

How Incisiv sounds in every communication — from research reports and event presentations to social media and client emails. Our tone should feel consistent, credible, and human.

Adaptable
We flex our voice to suit the audience — authoritative in research, warm at events, energetic on social. Confidence without rigidity.
Purposeful
Every word earns its place. We communicate with clarity and intent — no filler, no jargon for its own sake.
Collaborative
We speak with our audience, not at them. We invite dialogue, acknowledge different perspectives, and champion shared progress.
Future-Driven
We lead with what's next. Our language is forward-looking, optimistic, and grounded in the real forces shaping the industry.
2.0 Logo
2.1 Logo Elements

The Incisiv logo has two distinct components. The logomark is the abstract symbol — a cluster of connected nodes that represent data, connectivity, and insight. The logotype is the wordmark "INCISIV" set in wide-tracked, bold capital letters. Together they form the primary logo lockup, which is the default version for all communications.

Logomark
The iconic symbol on its own. Use only as a compact identifier — in favicons, social media avatars, or where the brand is already well-established.
Logotype
The wordmark "INCISIV" in custom typographic form. Wide letter-spacing and bold weight ensure maximum clarity and impact across all sizes.
Primary Logo
Logomark and logotype combined into a single lockup. This is the default version — use it unless space constraints require the logomark alone.
2.2 Clearspace

Clearspace is the minimum protected area around the logo. This breathing room must be kept free of any other visual elements — text, imagery, or graphics — to preserve the logo's prominence and impact. The minimum clearspace equals the height of the logomark on all four sides.

Rule
Maintain clear space equal to the height of the logomark on all sides. Never crowd the logo with other elements.
Applies to
All logo versions — primary lockup, logomark only, and logotype only.
2.3 Minimum Size

To ensure legibility, never reproduce the logo smaller than the sizes specified below. Going below these thresholds causes the logomark detail to break down and the logotype to become illegible.

Element Minimum Print Minimum Digital Use Case
Logomark only 10mm 50px Favicons, social media avatars, tight spaces
Logotype only 50mm 150px When brand context is already established
Full logo 50mm 150px Default usage across all materials
2.4 Logo on Backgrounds

The logo is designed for versatility across light and dark backgrounds. Always select a background that provides sufficient contrast. Use the white logo on dark backgrounds (black, navy) and the black logo on light backgrounds (white, light gray). Never place the logo on a busy or low-contrast background.

On White
#ffffff
On Black
#000000
On Navy
#131c4e
On Light Gray
#dbdbdb
2.5 Logo Misuse

To protect brand integrity, the logo must never be altered in any way. Avoid stretching, rotating, re-coloring, or adding effects. Each misuse example below shows what to avoid — proper and consistent use is essential to maintaining Incisiv's visual identity.

Do not stretch or distort
Do not rotate at any angle
Do not place in a container or badge
Do not flip horizontally
Do not flip vertically
Do not skew or shear the logo
3.0 Colors
3.1 Color Palette

The Incisiv color system is organized into three tiers. Each tier has a defined role — use them in the right proportion to maintain a consistent, professional brand presence. Click any swatch to copy its hex value.

Primary Dominant color. Used for cover backgrounds, primary headlines, and the logo lockup.

3.2 Color Usage Guidance

How to apply the palette correctly across materials.

Black
Primary color. Use for cover backgrounds, primary headlines on white, and the logo. Dominant — drives authority and confidence.
Navy #131c4e
Secondary color. Use for section headers, chapter backgrounds, dark UI components, and the circle motif on black. The brand's "workhorse" color.
Purple #7B2D8B
Tertiary accent. Used as headline/emphasis color in research reports and event materials. Never use as a large background. Pairs with navy for headings.
Dark Teal #14314D
Tertiary accent. Deep directional tone for gradient backgrounds paired with navy. Use in report cards, callout boxes, and dark UI components.
Orange #FA9628
Tertiary accent. High-energy accent for CTAs, data callout numbers, event highlights, and interactive UI elements. Use sparingly — maximum one element per layout.
Neutrals
Dark Gray for secondary body text. Mid Gray for metadata and captions. Light Gray for subtle dividers and backgrounds. White for light surfaces and reversed text.
4.0 Typography
4.1 Typefaces

Two typefaces make up the Incisiv type system. Poppins is used exclusively for all headlines, display text, and large callouts — its geometric, rounded letterforms convey confidence and modernity. Libre Franklin is used for all body copy, UI elements, captions, and buttons — its neutral, legible forms ensure readability at any size. Never substitute these fonts.

Poppins Headlines, Display, UI Labels
Aa Bb Cc Dd
ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ
abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz
0123456789 & ! @ # $ % ( ) + = . , ?
Light 300 Regular 400 Medium 500 SemiBold 600 Bold 700 ExtraBold 800
Libre Franklin Body Text, Captions, Buttons, UI
Aa Bb Cc Dd
ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ
abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz
0123456789 & ! @ # $ % ( ) + = . , ?
Regular 400 Medium 500 SemiBold 600 Italic
4.2 Type Hierarchy

Every text element has a defined role. The left column shows the role name, font, and exact specs. The right column shows a live specimen rendered in that exact style. Use this as your reference for every Incisiv material — from research reports and presentations to social posts and email templates.

Heading 1
Poppins Bold
Size: 40px / 2.5rem
Weight: 700
Line-height: 1.15
Tracking: -0.02em

Use for: Page titles, report headers, hero headlines
The Future of Retail Intelligence

Weight: 600

Size: 18px / 1.125rem
Weight: 500
Line-height: 1.4

Use for: Sub-sections, callout titles, sidebar headers

Libre Franklin Regular
Size: 16px / 1rem
Weight: 400
Line-height: 1.75

Incisiv's research is built on rigorous methodology and deep industry expertise. We deliver actionable insights that help retail and consumer leaders accelerate transformation and drive measurable business outcomes across every channel.
Caption
Libre Franklin Regular
Size: 13px / 0.8125rem
Weight: 400
Line-height: 1.6
Color: #999999

Use for: Image captions, footnotes, metadata
Source: Incisiv 2026 State of Retail Technology Report. Based on responses from 300+ retail executives across North America and Europe.
Label / Tag
Libre Franklin SemiBold
Size: 11px / 0.6875rem
Weight: 600
Letter-spacing: 0.1em
All caps: YES

Use for: Section labels, tags, category markers

Button

Size: 14px
Weight: 600
Border-radius: 4px
Padding: 10px 24px

5.0 Visual Element
5.1 Icons

All Incisiv icons use a consistent outline/line style — never filled. Stroke weight: 1.5px. Rounded line caps and joins. Always monochrome — match the icon color to the text color of its surface (dark on light, light on dark). Do not mix outline icons with filled icons in the same layout.

Phone
Web
Location
Email
Person
Search
Clock
Message
Settings
Network
Trust
Store
Screen
Analytics
6.0 Corporate Assets
6.1 Letterhead

Use the official letterhead template for all formal correspondence — proposals, agreements, and official communications. Logo placed top-left. Body text set in Libre Franklin Regular 11pt. Contact information and footer set in the bottom margin. Always print on white stock.

6.2 Business Card

Standard business card dimensions: 85mm × 55mm (print) / 1020px × 660px (digital at 300dpi). The front uses a black background with the white logo centered and the circle motif as a decorative element. The back is white — logo left-aligned, contact details set in Libre Franklin with name in Poppins Bold.

FRONT
Holder Name
Position / Title
hello@incisiv.com
+1 000 000 000
incisiv.com
7.0 Photography
7.1 Photo Direction

Photography shapes how audiences perceive Incisiv. Our imagery is precise, professional, and grounded in the real world of retail and commerce. All photos should feel forward-looking and credible — not generic stock. Three primary photography directions are used across all materials.

Direction 01
Aerial & Architectural
Top-down views of infrastructure and logistics. Creates a sense of scale, precision, and command.
Cool-toned Sharp focus Wide angle
Direction 02
Infrastructure & Supply Chain
Elevated perspectives on transportation, distribution, and commerce networks. Forward-looking and precise.
Dramatic scale Geometric Landscape
Direction 03
Technology & Retail Environments
Automated and organised retail spaces. Symmetrical, clean, forward-looking. Cool or neutral tones preferred.
Symmetrical Organised Cool tones
7.2 Do's & Don'ts
✓ Do
  • ✓Use bright, high-contrast images with clear subject matter
  • ✓Use aerial or architectural photography for editorial contexts
  • ✓Use imagery that connects to retail, technology, and commerce
  • ✓Maintain consistent cool-toned color treatment across all imagery
  • ✓Prefer wide, landscape-oriented images for editorial use
✕ Don't
  • ✕Use blurry, motion-blur, or out-of-focus photography
  • ✕Use heavily crowded or cluttered scenes without clear focus
  • ✕Use warm, oversaturated, or heavily filtered images
  • ✕Use generic stock imagery that feels impersonal
  • ✕Mix photography styles within the same document
8.0 Applications
8.1 Social Media Cards

Stat cards are Incisiv's primary social format — used across LinkedIn, Instagram, and Twitter/X to share research findings. Structure: logo top-left, large bold statistic (Poppins 800), one-line descriptor, source/report footer. Use navy, black, or navy-to-teal gradient backgrounds. Always include the white logo. Never place colored text on light backgrounds.

Post 1 — Market Snapshot: Frictionless Financial Services
Post 2 — Market Snapshot: When Personalization Stops Being Personal
FormatDimensionsBackgroundNotes
LinkedIn post1200 × 628pxDark Teal / NavySingle stat, source footer, logo top-left
Instagram square1080 × 1080pxDark Teal / NavyLarge stat, minimal text, illustration right
Twitter/X card1600 × 900pxDark Teal / NavyWide format, stat left-aligned, white logo
8.2 Website

Incisiv's web presence uses a dark navy foundation with white typography, structured grid layouts, and purple accents — maintaining a professional, research-led tone. The layout is clean and content-first, built to communicate credibility and depth.

Hero — Navigation & headline
Content — What to Expect & stats
Sessions & Keynotes — Agenda grid
Can't-Miss Events & CTA footer
